served_surgattn_specoff.label: surgattn force-2D M=1 AR ref, SERVED

finding: force-2D restores byte-exact run-to-run determinism in EAGER (min_frac=1.0, confirming #761's M-invariance locus) but NOT in the SERVED CUDA-graph path (spec-on min_frac=0.1875 => 26/32 prompts diverge run-to-run, M=1 AR ref min_frac=0.5 => 16/32). DECISIVE CONTRAST: full BI=1 batchinv IS served self-deterministic (min_frac=1.0, 0/32 divergent, 154.01 TPS) on the SAME CUDA-graph/inductor path. So the served run-to-run nondeterminism is NOT the compilation layer (batchinv shares it and reproduces) -- it is the BI=0 fast-path reductions (matmul/norm) that global batch-invariance freezes but the attention-only force-2D patch leaves untouched. The #761 attention locus is necessary-but-insufficient for served byte-exactness: the surgical revert recovers the speed (211.42 vs 154.01 TPS) but forfeits the byte-exact property, so it is NOT a strict-quality-safe replacement for batchinv.
